title: Replace pipe shell fallback with real PTY terminal provider
description: PROBLEM: Current fallback shell proves websocket transport but behaves poorly: no real PTY semantics, job-control warnings, weak interactive behavior. Wetty demonstrates the right architecture: real PTY + xterm + websocket. SUCCESS: Local provider uses a real PTY implementation when shell provider is explicitly enabled; falls back to disabled state if PTY dependency is unavailable; cwd/shell/env/rate-limit/security gates remain enforced. SCOPE: provider-registry/local PTY integration, dependency decision, graceful unavailable status, tests. NON_GOALS: SSH/Tailscale/tmux, multi-user auth beyond current gates. VALIDATION: terminal can run pwd/echo/interactive input without job-control warnings; provider tests pass; security tests retained.

title: Harden TerminalStream xterm lifecycle, focus, and resize
description: PROBLEM: Terminal UI throws xterm renderer dimension errors and input can stop working due React rerenders/remount/focus/fit races. SUCCESS: xterm is created once per mount, callbacks stay fresh without remount, focus works on click/open, fit is deferred until visible, drawer close/open does not trigger renderer exceptions. SCOPE: TerminalStream lifecycle, FitAddon timing, ResizeObserver, tests for input/focus/remount/zero-size behavior. NON_GOALS: backend/provider changes. VALIDATION: no browser console xterm dimension errors; focused TerminalStream/TerminalTabPanel tests pass; manual typing works.

title: Stabilize Gitboard dev server and websocket proxy
description: PROBLEM: Browser can hit stale/wrong backend/frontend processes and Vite did not proxy /api/console/terminal/ws as websocket, causing disconnected terminal and project load confusion. SUCCESS: One documented dev startup path runs backend and dashboard from current worktree; /api/github/repos loads via Vite; /api/console/terminal/status works; /api/console/terminal/ws upgrades through Vite; no stale IPv6/old-main process confusion. SCOPE: vite.config proxy, dev scripts/docs or guards, startup verification commands. VALIDATION: HTTP/API/WS smoke checks from frontend port. NON_GOALS: production deployment redesign.

title: Terminal UX recovery and Wetty-style PTY hardening
description: PROBLEM: Post-merge terminal support is functionally unstable and visually poor. User reports project loading regressions, disconnected/invalid-message states, xterm renderer crashes, and no usable input. code-server is a broad UX benchmark; Wetty is the direct terminal-over-web reference. SUCCESS: Gitboard has a reliable, good-looking terminal drawer using a real PTY-style backend, stable websocket/protocol path, robust xterm lifecycle/focus/fit behavior, and reproducible manual/automated smoke validation. SCOPE: Decompose and fix terminal/runtime regressions from current xt/yz1j work. Cover dev server/proxy consistency, provider architecture, xterm UI/lifecycle, security invariants, and smoke tests. NON_GOALS: No full VS Code clone, no SSH/Tailscale/tmux unless explicitly planned later, no broad app redesign. VALIDATION: project APIs load, terminal connects, accepts input, shows output, survives drawer close/reopen, no xterm renderer crashes, focused tests and manual smoke pass.

title: Fix post-merge Gitboard loading and terminal UI regressions
description: PROBLEM: After terminal E2E merge, user reports projects do not load and Terminal UI looks wrong/broken on dev server. SUCCESS: Gitboard loads project data again and Terminal tab styling/functionality is consistent with app design. SCOPE: Diagnose current xt/yz1j server/client errors, project-loading APIs/UI, TerminalTabPanel/TerminalStream styling/wiring as needed. NON_GOALS: no broad redesign, no unrelated backend refactor. CONSTRAINTS: Manual orchestrator intervention authorized by user; still keep changes minimal and run focused validation. VALIDATION: dev server logs clean of relevant errors; project endpoints/load path works; terminal visual/functionality smoke; focused tests. OUTPUT: files changed, errors found/fixed, validation.

Terminal wiring fail security review. Main break: session ownership removed, replaced with bearer-style `sessionId`/`streamId` control. Persisted `sessionId` in `localStorage` makes reuse easier. Merge blocked pending fixes.

## Status

- Verdict: FAIL
- Merge readiness: not ready

## Findings

- ID: TERM-001
- Severity: high
- Category/CWE when applicable: CWE-384 Session Fixation / CWE-639 Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
- Evidence:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts:103-155` — `attach`, `input`, `resize`, `exit` no longer check connection ownership; only `sessionId` + `streamId`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts` diff vs `xt/yz1j` removes `ownerConnectionId` gate
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/TerminalTabPanel.tsx:34-43,84-91,103` — client uses same value for `streamId` and `sessionId`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts:55-56,155-165` — `terminalSessionId` persisted in `localStorage`
  - `apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/terminal.test.ts:67-86` — test changed from deny cross-connection control to allow reattach/control from new connection
- Reachability/exploitability:
  - same-origin tab/script can read persisted `terminalSessionId`
  - same-origin tab/script can open websocket, send `attach`, then `input`/`resize`/`exit`
  - no server-side bind to original websocket or user-specific secret in scoped code
- Impact:
  - PTY session hijack
  - command injection into already-open shell
  - forced resize/exit
  - cross-tab misuse exactly within task threat model
- Recommended fix:
  - restore server-side ownership proof for mutating actions
  - if reattach needed, use server-minted unguessable reattach token separate from `sessionId`
  - bind reattach token to authenticated user/session and expire on detach/timeout
  - keep `streamId` distinct from authorization secret
  - add negative tests: foreign connection cannot control session without valid reattach credential
- Verification after fix:
  - test that second websocket without reattach token gets `forbidden`
  - test that stolen `sessionId` from `localStorage` alone cannot `attach`/`input`/`exit`
  - test legit reconnect with valid server-issued token still works

- ID: TERM-002
- Severity: medium
- Category/CWE when applicable: CWE-922 Insecure Storage of Sensitive Information / CWE-200 Exposure of Sensitive Information
- Evidence:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts:55-56` — rehydrates `terminalSessionId` and `terminalOutput` from `localStorage`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/stores/shell.ts:157,164,170` — writes live session identifier and terminal output to `localStorage`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/TerminalTabPanel.tsx:55-57` — all output appended for persistence
- Reachability/exploitability:
  - terminal output often contains repo paths, logs, tokens, command results
  - data survives tab close/reopen and browser restarts
  - same-origin script, shared workstation user, browser extension with storage access can recover data
  - persisted `terminalSessionId` extends TERM-001 hijack window during 30s cleanup
- Impact:
  - sensitive terminal transcript at rest in browser
  - easier live-session reattach after tab close/remount
- Recommended fix:
  - do not persist live `terminalSessionId` in `localStorage`
  - avoid persisting terminal output by default; keep in memory or use explicit opt-in encrypted/session-scoped cache
  - clear output/session immediately on detach, drawer close, logout, provider exit
  - if persistence required, redact/size-limit by bytes and use `sessionStorage` minimum
- Verification after fix:
  - inspect browser storage after terminal use; no live session credential persisted
  - confirm terminal transcript absent after reload unless explicit secure opt-in
  - test drawer remount path using secure reattach token flow, not `localStorage` session reuse

CONSTRAINTS: Use specialists-v3 workflow. Run specialists list --full before dispatch. Re-read target bead before each chain. Use executor -> reviewer for implementation chains. Run security-auditor before any PTY/shell-capable code lands. Keep TerminalStream renderer separate from provider/session ownership. Treat shell exposure as high-risk.

ATTACK PLAN:

   - Target bead: forge-lqyc.2 Define terminal stream protocol.

title: Drawer height ignored by flex:1 on .ide-main children — opt drawer out
description:
## PROBLEM
globals.css line 4387: `.ide-main > * { flex: 1; min-height: 0; min-width: 0; }` applies `flex: 1` to ALL direct children of .ide-main, including the new `<BottomDrawer>`. flex:1 (shorthand for `flex: 1 1 0%`) overrides any `style={{ height: <px> }}` set inline. Result: BottomDrawer's height-driven resize / maximize / 75vh-default all UPDATE the store + inline style correctly but the RENDERED visual height never changes — flex distributes available space proportionally instead.

Operator-confirmed via Firefox DevTools console: move/maximize handlers fire with correct math (h=106..139 as expected, maximize toggle logs show new heights) but the drawer doesn't visually resize.

Playwright tests missed this because we only checked `element.style.height` (the inline style we wrote), never `getBoundingClientRect().height` (what flex actually renders).

## SUCCESS
- Visual drawer height matches the value set via store (drag changes visible height in real time, maximize jumps, 75vh default opens at ~75vh).
- `getBoundingClientRect().height` ≈ store drawerHeight ±2px (the ±2 accounts for sub-pixel rounding).
- Also: simultaneously fix sibling `.ide-main-content` to claim the remaining flex space (flex:1) so MainPane content stays scrollable. Without this the content area would have undefined height once drawer opts out.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css
  - Add after line 4391: `.ide-main > .bottom-drawer { flex: 0 0 auto; }` — drawer claims explicit pixel height only.
  - Keep `.ide-main > * { flex: 1; ... }` for `.ide-main-content` so it expands.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawer.tsx
  - REMOVE the three console.log diagnostic lines (operator confirmed module load works; logs are noise now).

## NON_GOALS
- No changes to drawer JSX, no changes to resize handler, no changes to store.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: no gitnexus_* calls.

## VALIDATION
- bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean.
- Playwright sanity: open drawer, getBoundingClientRect().height should equal the store drawerHeight value. Then drag, recheck — rect.height changes. Maximize — rect.height = viewport - 24.

## OUTPUT
- 2-file diff
- Confirmation that .bottom-drawer flex: 0 0 auto is set
- Confirmation that diagnostic console.log lines removed
notes:
Removed drawer diagnostic logs; opted .bottom-drawer out of .ide-main flex:1.
### Specialist Output — executor (job d6e00d) [WAITING — more output may follow]

title: Attack plan: backend/observability/deploy sprint sequencing
description:
PURPOSE
Single source of truth for next-session execution order across the 13 open beads as of 2026-05-18. Captures triage decisions, parallel-track structure, and the rationale so any future agent (or this one cold-starting) can resume without re-deriving the plan.

CURRENT OPEN BEADS (13)
- forge-ci9   P1  Visual fix pass (collapse/black-bg) — SUSPECTED STALE post-shell-rewrite
- forge-8kdc  P2  Dockerize gitboard as VPS service + Tailscale access
- forge-a72   P2  [bug] Baseline tsc cleanup (28 errors) — unblocks sp merge gate
- forge-ftj   P2  [bug] Beads Feed ready/blocked — SUSPECTED DUPLICATE of closed forge-kz7
- forge-wvlj  P2  Dashboard bottom log drawer (UI) — blocked by forge-zpw8
- forge-zpw8  P2  Backend event-log emitter + WS feed + 7-day disk retention
- forge-1fb   P3  Beadboard bv parity gaps in native scoring
- forge-6q1   P3  [bug] Store release.tag_name separately in github_events
- forge-che   P3  Console: stats from observability.db
- forge-e23   P3  Console: Specialists tab (live job list + chain history)
- forge-ebr   P3  Add focused Beads Feed tests
- forge-0up   P4  DEFERRED: Inline EventDetail in sidebar
- forge-3r1   P4  DEFERRED: ContributionHeatmap in sidebar

STEP 0 — Triage pass (do FIRST, before claiming any work)
- forge-ci9: bd show + diff intent against current apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/{Sidebar,TopBar,MainPane}.tsx. If shell rewrite covers the goals, bd close with reason 'superseded by forge-7xu / forge-ci9 phase 2 shell rewrite'. Memory ack: 'nothing novel'.
- forge-ftj: bd show + compare scope against closed forge-kz7. The closed bead derived blocked/ready from dep graph in IssueFeed; if ftj is the same scope, close as duplicate. Memory ack: 'nothing novel'.
- Expected outcome: ~11 active beads.

STEP 1 — Two parallel tracks (disjoint file scopes; safe to run concurrently)

TRACK A: observability/deploy
  Order  Bead          Why this slot                                              Estimate
  A.1    forge-a72     Highest leverage — unblocks sp merge gate for every       half day
                       future chain. We manually merged 7 commits this session
                       because sp merge tsc-gates on these 28 baseline errors.
  A.2a   forge-zpw8    Backend logger; touches src/core/*, src/api/ws/*,         1 day
                       src/types/*. No frontend deps. CAN start in parallel
                       with A.2b — disjoint scope from Docker.
  A.3    forge-wvlj    UI drawer; depends on forge-zpw8 (consumes its event      1 day
                       names + endpoint shape). Sequential after A.2a.

TRACK B: parallel with track A
  Order  Bead          Why this slot                                              Estimate
  A.2b   forge-8kdc    Docker + Tailscale. Touches root (Dockerfile,              half day
                       compose, .dockerignore, docs/). Disjoint from forge-zpw8.
                       References /data/logs path that zpw8 also targets, but
                       zpw8 has a ./logs fallback constraint so either order
                       lands clean.
  ANY    forge-ebr     Small focused tests; can slot in during any reviewer      1-2 hr
                       wait window in either track.

PARALLEL DISPATCH RULE
- bd commit-gate is project-wide. Parallel executors are fine; the SECOND commit waits until the first chain closes + commits. Plan accordingly: close one chain's bead before starting another's commit.

STEP 2 — Console mini-epic (next session+1)
- forge-che + forge-e23 together form a coherent 'console = observability surface' feature.
- RECOMMENDATION: promote to an epic with these two as children; consider adding 1-2 more (e.g. live realtime metrics graph backed by forge-zpw8's log stream) for a complete surface.
- Lower urgency than tracks above; the log drawer (forge-wvlj) already gives operator-visible observability at the dashboard level.

STEP 3 — Long-tail (claim opportunistically)
- forge-6q1: small data-shape fix, ~1 hr; isolated to github-store.ts + tests.
- forge-1fb: scoring parity; needs domain question before scope is firm — ask operator before claiming.
- forge-0up / forge-3r1: stay deferred (P4) until operator asks.

RECOMMENDED NEXT-SESSION OPENING MOVE
1. Run Step 0 triage (close ci9 + ftj if confirmed superseded/duplicate).
2. Claim forge-a72 + forge-8kdc; dispatch both executors with --worktree --keep-alive --background.
3. Monitor per v3 cadence; close them one at a time.
4. Then claim forge-zpw8 (longest single bead, ~10 files, needs careful call-site instrumentation pass).
5. Then forge-wvlj.

CONTEXT THIS BEAD PRESERVES
- Why forge-a72 is P0-priority-effectively (compounds across all future merges).
- Why ci9 + ftj are 'suspected superseded/duplicate' rather than immediately closed (operator should confirm with current code state).
- Why Track A and Track B parallelize cleanly (disjoint file scopes + tsc-gate-only dependency).
- Why forge-wvlj sequential not parallel with forge-zpw8 (consumes its event-name contract).
- Why forge-che + forge-e23 want to be an epic (single coherent surface).

NON-GOALS for this bead
- Not a planning surface for individual bead scope changes — those edits go on the beads themselves.
- Not a status tracker — close the implementation beads as they ship; this bead can be closed when the Step 1 tracks complete.

CLOSE WHEN
- forge-a72, forge-zpw8, forge-wvlj, forge-8kdc all closed AND triage on forge-ci9 + forge-ftj completed.

REFERENCES
- This conversation's session report (will be generated next): .xtrm/reports/2026-05-18-<sha>.md
- Existing memories: realtime-envelope-uses-boot-id, realtime-envelope-version-is-string, beads-watcher-diffs-4-entity-snapshots, dolt-connection-stability-forge-1qz, beadschangewatcher-fast-path-forge-3mdw
notes:
UPDATE 2026-05-18 post-PR-#10 verification: Codex review surfaced 4 production bugs in realtime stack (filed as forge-yy27, P1). This becomes the new HIGHEST-PRIORITY implementation work — supersedes forge-a72 as Step 1.A.1. Reason: live Beads sync is silently dead in production today (Fix 1 in forge-yy27); GitHub backfill silently truncates for any repo with >100 issues (Fix 2 + 3); sync_hint recovery never runs (Fix 4). All shipped via PR #10 merged 2026-05-18 20:53 UTC. None of the reviewer-PASS chains in this session caught them because review focused on contract conformance, not on consumer-side envelope unwrapping or pagination retention.

REVISED STEP 1 ORDER:
  A.0    forge-yy27   P1  Realtime production bug fixes (NEW — was not in original plan)
  A.1    forge-a72    P2  tsc cleanup (still high leverage — sp merge gate)
  A.2a   forge-zpw8   P2  Backend logger
  A.2b   forge-8kdc   P2  Docker + Tailscale
  A.3    forge-wvlj   P2  UI drawer (blocked by zpw8)

PROCESS LESSON
Reviewer contract: from now on, contract-conformance reviewers must ALSO check at least one consumer-side integration test for the new envelope shape, not just type+payload alignment. Otherwise drift between producer and consumer slips silently. Update reviewer bead template accordingly when running observability/realtime work.

description: PROBLEM: forge-px57 restitched forge-lqyc.4 terminal WebSocket bridge after the original executor failed. The bridge touches browser-to-backend terminal message handling, provider registry gating, session lifecycle, input forwarding, output streaming, and server websocket dispatch; it must be security-audited before merge. SCOPE: Debugger job ed81e9 diff/worktree, especially apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts, apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts, apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/provider-registry.ts, apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/terminal.test.ts, and interactions with packages/core terminal protocol and shell-provider-policy/local-pty-provider. Scoped security audit done for terminal websocket bridge. Reviewed apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts, apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts, provider-registry.ts, protocol.ts, shell-provider-policy.ts, local-pty-provider.ts, related tests. Ran bun test for terminal/shell/local-pty/protocol suites: 21 pass. Found authz gap in TerminalBridge: attach/input/resize/exit trust only client-supplied sessionId, no owner/attachment/stream binding; open also allows duplicate sessionId overwrite without disposing prior session.
### Specialist Output — security-auditor (job 378b5f) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Summary

FINDINGS. Merge not security-OK yet.

## Status

- Scope reviewed:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/provider-registry.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/core/shell-provider-policy.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/src/core/local-pty-provider.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/terminal.test.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/shell.test.ts`
  - `apps/gitboard/tests/core/local-pty-provider.test.ts`
  - `packages/core/src/terminal/protocol.ts`
  - `packages/core/tests/terminal-protocol.test.ts`
- Overall risk: medium
- Mode(s) used:
  - static secure-code review
  - bounty-style exploitability triage
- Commands/sources used:
  - `sed`, `nl`, `rg`, `git diff`, `git status`
  - `bun test apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/terminal.test.ts apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/shell.test.ts apps/gitboard/tests/core/local-pty-provider.test.ts packages/core/tests/terminal-protocol.test.ts`

## Changes

None. Scan-only.

## Verification

- Shared protocol validation present. `packages/core/src/terminal/protocol.ts:206-254`, `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts:35-45`
- Disabled/default-safe provider gating present. `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/provider-registry.ts:24-49`
- Native PTY safe-disabled when unavailable. `apps/gitboard/src/core/local-pty-provider.ts:295-302`
- Shell websocket policy gate present for shell path only. `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:127-138`, `apps/gitboard/src/core/shell-provider-policy.ts:103-105`
- Targeted tests pass. `21 pass, 0 fail`

## Risks

### Finding 1
- ID: TERM-WS-001
- Severity: medium
- Category/CWE: CWE-285 Improper Authorization
- Evidence:
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts:69-98` — `attach`, `input`, `resize`, `exit` authorize by `sessionId` lookup only
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts:48-63` — `open` trusts client-chosen `sessionId`, stores session under that key
  - `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/bridge.ts:76-79` — `detach` removes attachment for any supplied `sessionId`
  - `apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/terminal.test.ts:27-66` — lifecycle tests cover happy path + disabled provider only; no cross-connection ownership tests
- Reachability/exploitability:
  - Any client on `/api/console/terminal/ws` can send `attach`, `input`, `resize`, `exit` with guessed or reused `sessionId`
  - No check that sender opened session
  - No check that sender remains attached
  - No check that `streamId` matches stored session stream
- Impact:
  - If any interactive provider later becomes enabled, one websocket client can hijack, drive, or terminate another client session
  - Duplicate `open` on same `sessionId` can overwrite map entry, orphan old session until provider exits/disconnects
- Recommended fix:
  - Bind session ownership to server-issued opaque handle, not client-chosen `sessionId` alone
  - Reject `attach/input/resize/exit/detach` unless connection owns session or presents validated attach token
  - Reject duplicate `open` for existing `sessionId`, or dispose old session before replace
  - Enforce `streamId === state.streamId` on mutating messages
  - Add tests for cross-connection attach/input/exit denial and duplicate-session rejection
- Verification after fix:
  - Add tests where `connB` cannot control `connA` session
  - Add test for duplicate `open` same `sessionId`
  - Re-run terminal route suite

## Non-findings / Dropped Noise

- No user input/output logging found in audited bridge path. `bridge.ts` logs nothing. `server.ts` request logs keep path/status/error only. `apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:56-67`
- Provider registry does not bypass shell policy today. `pty` remains disabled even when shell policy enabled. `apps/gitboard/src/api/terminal/provider-registry.ts:34-40`
- Native PTY default stays safe-disabled when dependency unavailable. `apps/gitboard/src/core/local-pty-provider.ts:295-302`
- Shared protocol validation exists, but only shape/type validation. Not enough for ownership/authz. `packages/core/src/terminal/protocol.ts:206-254`

## Follow-ups

- Debugger fix `TerminalBridge` session authorization model.
- Add negative tests for:
  - invalid/duplicate `sessionId`
  - cross-connection attach

title: Validate terminal stream architecture and safety design
description:
PROBLEM: The epic's highest-risk design choices need critique before implementation starts: reusable TerminalStream boundaries, generic stream protocol shape, provider/session ownership, local PTY safety, optional tmux persistence, future SSH/Tailscale scope, and specialist-feed reuse.
SUCCESS: Overthinker either validates the current architecture or proposes sharper boundaries/sequence changes with clear tradeoffs and updated implementation guidance.
SCOPE: Beads forge-lqyc, forge-lqyc.1 through forge-lqyc.11; current Gitboard architecture only as needed for design fit. Focus on contracts and design, not code edits.
NON_GOALS: No implementation; no broad codebase audit; no decision to make tmux mandatory; no SSH implementation plan beyond future-provider boundaries.
CONSTRAINTS: READ_ONLY. Treat raw shell exposure as high-risk. Keep terminal rendering separate from process ownership. Prefer MVP that ships local/dev PTY safely while preserving future provider extensibility.
VALIDATION: Findings should answer: are child bead scopes sequenced correctly; is provider abstraction too broad/too narrow; are safety gates sufficient; should specialist-feed be in MVP or follow-up; what should change before executor dispatch.
OUTPUT: PASS/ADJUST recommendation, revised design notes, dependency/order changes if needed, and explicit next beads safe to start.
acceptance_criteria: Overthinker reviews the epic and critical child beads; validates or revises provider abstraction; validates or revises stream protocol boundaries; validates or revises shell safety model; evaluates whether specialist-feed belongs in MVP path; confirms tmux is optional; identifies any missing child beads/dependencies; returns actionable changes before implementation begins.
notes:
### Specialist Output — overthinker (job 9696a7) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Summary

Recommendation: **ADJUST**.

Architecture direction good. Boundaries need sharper before executor dispatch. Biggest risk: provider/session layer can become remote shell API too early. MVP must ship local/dev PTY with strict safety gates, renderer/process split, future providers behind narrow contract.

## Status

Phase work complete. No files changed. GitNexus used before code evidence. `bd` claim/close not run: no shell tool available in harness.

## Phase 1 - Initial Analysis

Goal: validate terminal stream design before implementation.

Known repo fit:
- Existing realtime stack has `WsClient`, shared subscriptions, resume, sequence tracking, boot id.
- Server side has `ChannelRegistry` with per-channel sequence, ring buffer, replay gap detection.
- Specialist routes expose job/chains/in-flight state; no process ownership there.
- No terminal implementation found in visible evidence.

Core design should split into three layers:

1. **TerminalStream UI**
   - Renders bytes.
   - Sends input/resize intents.
   - Knows `sessionId`, `streamId`, protocol version.
   - Does not know shell, cwd, spawn, tmux, SSH, specialist ownership.

2. **Transport/protocol**
   - WebSocket channel or session-scoped stream.
   - Discriminated event union.
   - Supports output, input, resize, exit, error, snapshot, heartbeat, backpressure.

3. **Provider/session owner**
   - Owns PTY/tmux/future SSH lifecycle.
   - Applies safety policy.
   - Enforces cwd/session limits/kill behavior.
   - Emits stream events.

## Phase 2 - Devil's Advocate

Main failure modes:

- **Generic stream protocol too broad**
  - If protocol tries to cover terminal, logs, specialist feed, SSH, tmux all at once, MVP stalls.
  - Fix: generic envelope, terminal-specific payloads. No generic RPC.

- **Provider abstraction too early**
  - If provider interface includes SSH/Tailscale now, security model blurs.
  - Fix: `TerminalSessionProvider` narrow. Local provider only in MVP. Remote provider capability flags later.

- **Raw shell exposure high-risk**
  - Browser-to-shell path becomes critical attack surface.
  - Fix: opt-in dev gate, repo allowlist, command/session policy, audit, rate limits, timeouts.

- **Terminal rendering coupled to process ownership**
  - Reuse dies if component imports PTY/session APIs.
  - Fix: UI receives stream client props only.

- **tmux persistence can distort MVP**
  - Mandatory tmux adds install/runtime assumptions.
  - Fix: optional provider capability. PTY first. tmux follow-up.

- **Specialist-feed reuse may overfit**
  - Specialist output likely log/job stream, not interactive PTY.
  - Fix: reuse viewer shell/render frame later, not MVP process protocol.

## Phase 3 - Synthesis

ADJUST, not reject.

Use existing websocket patterns only for transport inspiration:
- Keep sequence/version/boot concepts.
- Do not force terminal onto existing `ChannelRegistry` replay semantics without byte-volume/backpressure review.
- Terminal output needs stronger flow control than system logs.

Revised MVP boundary:
- MVP = local provider + TerminalStream UI + terminal protocol + safety policy.
- Non-MVP = tmux persistence, SSH/Tailscale provider, specialist-feed integration.

Provider contract should be narrow:

```ts
type TerminalProviderCapability =
  | "interactive"
  | "resize"
  | "snapshot"
  | "persistence";

interface TerminalSessionProvider {
  kind: "local-pty" | "tmux" | "ssh";
  capabilities: TerminalProviderCapability[];

  create(request: CreateSessionRequest): Promise<TerminalSessionHandle>;
  attach(sessionId: string): AsyncIterable<TerminalStreamEvent>;
  input(sessionId: string, data: string): Promise<void>;
  resize(sessionId: string, cols: number, rows: number): Promise<void>;
  terminate(sessionId: string, reason: string): Promise<void>;
}
```

Safety gates required before shell launch:
- Explicit dev-mode config.
- Localhost/admin-only access.
- CWD constrained to assigned repo/worktree.
- Shell allowlist.
- Env scrub.
- Session count cap.
- Idle timeout and hard TTL.
- Input/output byte limits.
- Audit log for create/input/terminate.
- Orphan cleanup.
- No arbitrary command launch API in MVP unless allowlisted.

## Phase 4 - Final Refined Output

## Recommendation

**ADJUST**.

Current architecture can work if executor starts with contracts and safety, not terminal feature breadth.

## Revised Design Notes

- `TerminalStream` owns rendering only.
- `TerminalSessionProvider` owns PTY/tmux/SSH lifecycle.
- Protocol uses generic envelope + terminal event payloads.
- Local PTY provider only in MVP.
- tmux optional follow-up capability.
- SSH/Tailscale future provider only. No MVP route/code path.
- Specialist-feed reuse follow-up. Avoid blocking terminal MVP.

## Dependency / Order Changes

Recommended order:

1. **Protocol contract bead**
   - Event types, versioning, stream/session ids, error model, resize/input/output semantics.

2. **Safety policy bead**
   - Dev gate, cwd allowlist, shell allowlist, env scrub, caps, audit, cleanup.

3. **Renderer-only TerminalStream bead**
   - No provider/process imports.

4. **Local PTY provider bead**
   - Depends on protocol + safety.

5. **API/WebSocket integration bead**
   - Depends on provider and protocol.

6. **Optional tmux bead**
   - After PTY stable.

7. **Specialist-feed reuse bead**
   - After terminal renderer proves stable.

8. **SSH/Tailscale boundary bead**
   - Design-only later. No implementation now.

## Next Beads Safe To Start

Safe now:
- Bead scoped to terminal protocol/types.
- Bead scoped to safety policy/spec/tests.
- Bead scoped to renderer-only `TerminalStream`.

Not safe yet:
- Local PTY implementation before safety gates.
- tmux persistence before PTY contract stable.

title: Wire local terminal end-to-end
description:

NON_GOALS: No tmux; no SSH/Tailscale; no specialist-feed UI; no broad drawer redesign.
CONSTRAINTS: Respect safety gates. Close/open drawer must not immediately destroy the active session; hard browser/server disconnect should detach and schedule cleanup. Resize messages must flow after fit calculations.
VALIDATION: Manual or automated smoke: open Terminal, type a harmless command, observe output, resize drawer, close/reopen, confirm same session, exit, and build passes.
OUTPUT: Changed files, smoke/build evidence, remaining known limitations.
acceptance_criteria: Opening Terminal creates or attaches to local PTY session; typing in xterm sends input to PTY; PTY output appears in terminal; resizing drawer updates PTY size; close/reopen preserves active terminal session for configured grace period; disconnected/error/exit states are visible; build passes.

title: Add terminal WebSocket bridge
description:
PROBLEM: Browser terminal clients need a websocket bridge that maps generic terminal-stream messages to backend providers without hardcoding local PTY behavior into the frontend.
SUCCESS: Backend websocket/channel supports opening, attaching, detaching, input, output streaming, resize, exit, and error for terminal stream sessions.
SCOPE: Server websocket routing/channel; provider registry integration; session ID validation; disconnect cleanup; frontend transport client only if needed to exercise protocol.
NON_GOALS: No TerminalStream UI implementation; no provider-specific business logic beyond registry calls; no SSH/tmux implementation.
CONSTRAINTS: Reject invalid session IDs and disabled provider kinds safely. Detach on websocket disconnect and schedule disposal rather than leaking processes. Avoid logging user input. Surface provider errors as protocol error/status messages.
VALIDATION: Protocol tests cover open/attach/input/output/resize/exit/error/invalid session/disabled provider/disconnect cleanup using mocked providers.
OUTPUT: Websocket bridge files, protocol test evidence, cleanup behavior summary.
acceptance_criteria: Browser can open a terminal session; browser can attach to existing session; input messages write to provider; provider output streams back to frontend; resize messages resize provider session; exit/error messages are forwarded; disconnects detach or schedule cleanup; invalid session IDs are rejected safely; no orphaned processes after disconnect/timeout.

title: Implement local PTY provider
description:
PROBLEM: The drawer terminal needs a concrete server-side provider that owns a real local PTY process scoped to the current repo/workspace while exposing only the generic terminal-stream interface.
SUCCESS: Backend can create, resize, write to, emit output from, and dispose local PTY sessions, with lifecycle/idle tracking and tests using mocks where native PTY is hard to run in CI.
SCOPE: Backend terminal provider/session manager; repo-root cwd resolution; shell selection; lifecycle cleanup; unit tests/mocks. Prefer node-pty unless install/build constraints make that impractical.
NON_GOALS: No websocket protocol handling beyond provider interface; no tmux; no SSH; no production enablement bypass.
CONSTRAINTS: Spawn in repo/workspace root only. Default shell to user shell or bash. Never log command input or secrets. Provider must be disabled by safety config when not allowed. Clean up child processes on dispose, idle timeout, and server/session shutdown.
VALIDATION: Unit tests cover create, write, resize, output event, idle timeout, explicit dispose, and shell/cwd configuration using mocks where needed.
OUTPUT: Provider implementation, tests run, native dependency notes, residual platform risks.
acceptance_criteria: Uses node-pty or vetted equivalent; creates a terminal session with cwd set to repo/workspace root; spawns configured shell defaulting to user shell or bash; supports input writes; streams output chunks; supports resize rows/cols; tracks lifecycle and last activity; kills idle sessions after configured timeout; kills PTY on explicit close; lifecycle tests cover create/write/resize/dispose.

title: Define terminal stream protocol
description:
PROBLEM: Frontend and backend need a shared terminal stream contract before adding providers, otherwise drawer terminal, specialist feeds, tmux, and future SSH support will drift into incompatible one-off protocols.
SUCCESS: Shared protocol/types define a versioned terminal stream envelope, terminal-specific payloads, provider kinds, capabilities, lifecycle states, and websocket message shapes.
SCOPE: Shared TypeScript types/schema/validation utilities used by frontend and backend terminal code. Include protocol documentation close to the types.
NON_GOALS: No provider implementation; no websocket server wiring beyond types; no SSH/tmux implementation; no broad generic RPC framework.
CONSTRAINTS: Use a generic envelope plus terminal-specific payloads, not an over-broad stream RPC. Model provider kinds pty, tmux, ssh, command, specialist-feed. Capabilities must distinguish readonly vs interactive, resizable, snapshot, and persistent. Include stream/session ids, protocol version, error model, resize/input/output semantics, heartbeat or status as needed, and a backpressure/byte-volume note.
VALIDATION: Protocol validation tests cover open/attach/detach/input/output/resize/exit/error/status and reject invalid state transitions.
OUTPUT: Shared protocol files, documented message examples, validation evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Shared protocol types live in shared code; protocol supports provider capabilities readonly/interactive/resizable/persistent; message types include open, attach, detach, input, output, resize, exit, error, and status; invalid state transitions are rejected; frontend can render disabled/errored/exited states consistently; protocol is documented enough for future providers.

description:
PROBLEM: Gitboard needs a reusable terminal renderer that can display interactive shell output now and readonly/non-shell streams later without embedding provider-specific assumptions in UI code.
SUCCESS: A TerminalStream frontend component wraps xterm.js and exposes a provider-agnostic API for bytes in, optional input out, resize events, attach/detach lifecycle, status rendering, and styling.
SCOPE: Frontend terminal component and any minimal styles/tests needed for static rendering. Likely areas: drawer/component tree, shared UI components, test mocks for xterm and @xterm/addon-fit.
NON_GOALS: No websocket implementation; no PTY/tmux/SSH/session ownership logic; no Specialists tab redesign.
CONSTRAINTS: Component must support readonly mode for specialist feeds and read/write mode for shells. Keep API generic: callbacks/props should not mention PTY, tmux, SSH, or specialists. Cleanly dispose xterm and fit addon on unmount.
VALIDATION: Static render and resize smoke tests with xterm mocked; manual or test evidence that readonly mode blocks keyboard input while still rendering ANSI output.
OUTPUT: Component API, styling notes, tests run, and any residual integration risks.

title: Observability tokens read wrong JSON path (token_usage nested)
description:
## PROBLEM
metrics-dao.ts:240 reads token fields directly from each trajectory entry (input_tokens, output_tokens, etc.) but the actual JSON structure nests them under .token_usage:
  {"t":..., "source":"turn_end", "token_usage":{"input_tokens":11895, "output_tokens":114, "cache_creation_tokens":0, "cache_read_tokens":0, "total_tokens":12009}}
  {"turn_index":1, "t":..., "token_usage":{...}}
Result: every token table renders 0 across all specialists.

## FIX
In parseTokenTrajectory (line 236-247), for each item extract the nested token_usage object first:
  const item_record = firstRecord(item);
  const usage = firstRecord(item_record.token_usage);
Then read input_tokens / output_tokens / cache_creation_tokens / cache_read_tokens / total_tokens from `usage`. Keep the fallback math when total_tokens is missing.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/server/observability/metrics-dao.ts (parseTokenTrajectory only). ~3-line patch.

## CONSTRAINTS
- No gitnexus_* calls.

## VALIDATION
- bunx tsc clean.
- Manual orchestrator hits /api/console/observability/summary?range=all and verifies tokens.totals.total > 0.

forge-che shipped a metrics-dao.ts that queries every attached repo's specialist_job_metrics table. Some attached dbs (e.g. repo_barchart_scraper_1) don't have that table — the per-alias query throws SQLiteError 'no such table: <alias>.specialist_job_metrics' and 500s the whole /api/console/observability/summary endpoint.

Existing observability/dao.ts handles this safely because it queries specialist_jobs which all dbs have.

## SUCCESS

title: Firefox-safe drawer resize: simple onMouseDown + document listeners + diagnostic log
description:
## PROBLEM
Operator is on Firefox (lsof confirmed firefox connected to localhost:5173). All Playwright tests have run on headless Chromium — which is why every fix passed in tests but failed in the operator's real browser. Firefox has quirks with setPointerCapture and onPointerDown that Chromium doesn't share.

## SUCCESS
- Drag works in Firefox.
- Maximize works in Firefox.
- Diagnostic console.log lines at startResize entry, at each pointermove, and at toggleMaximize so the operator can paste the Firefox DevTools console output if it still fails.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawer.tsx
  - REVERT to a simple, cross-browser pattern based on onMouseDown (NOT onPointerDown):
    - onMouseDown on the resizer div.
    - Inside the handler: stop here if event.button !== 0. Then:
      - document.body.style.userSelect = 'none' (prevent text-selection drag from intercepting)
      - document.body.style.cursor = 'row-resize'
      - document.addEventListener('mousemove', onMove) — NOT window. Document fires reliably across Firefox/Chromium/Safari.
      - document.addEventListener('mouseup', onUp, { once: true }).
    - onMove: `setDrawerHeight(window.innerHeight - e.clientY)`. Add `console.log("[drawer] move", e.clientY, "→ h", newH)` (will be removed after operator confirms).
    - onUp: remove mousemove listener, restore body styles.
  - At module top: `console.log("[drawer] BottomDrawer module loaded — onMouseDown+document version A")` so operator can confirm in Firefox console which build is live after hard reload.
  - In toggleMaximize: `console.log("[drawer] toggleMaximize current=", currentHeight, "restored=", restoredHeight)`.
  - Drop setPointerCapture entirely — it is the Firefox-flaky part.
  - Drop the try/catch + document-fallback fork from forge-12mn — replace with single simple path.

- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css
  - Keep `touch-action: none` on `.bottom-drawer-resizer` (helpful for touchpad gestures).
  - Add `cursor: row-resize` (already there) and ensure no z-index weirdness blocking the handle.

## NON_GOALS
- No new features. No removal of maximize button. No styling rework.
- Do NOT use onPointerDown / setPointerCapture / window.addEventListener for this revision.
- Operator-side console.logs are intentional and stay until operator confirms drag works; we'll remove in a follow-up bead.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_*.
- Vite has been restarted; HMR is fresh. Tell the operator to do a hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R) to pick this up.

## VALIDATION
- bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean.
- Operator confirms: console shows `[drawer] BottomDrawer module loaded` on page load, and `[drawer] move ...` lines stream while they drag.

## OUTPUT
- One-file diff (BottomDrawer.tsx) + maybe globals.css unchanged.
- Confirmation that setPointerCapture is GONE.
- Confirmation that document.addEventListener (not window) is used.
- Confirmation that 3 console.log lines are present (module load, move, toggleMaximize).

title: Defensive fix: drawer resize fails for some real mice (StrictMode + preventDefault order + touch-action)
description:
## PROBLEM
After forge-wtkk switched to onPointerDown + setPointerCapture, real-pointer Playwright drag passes BUT the operator's real browser still does nothing on drag or maximize click.

Observed browser console error from a synthetic-PointerEvent probe:
  NotFoundError: Failed to execute 'setPointerCapture' on 'Element': No active pointer with the given id is found.
  at startResize (...:83:10)

Three compounding root causes:
1. apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/main.tsx wraps the app in `<StrictMode>`. In dev, JSX handlers are bound twice per render; under some pointer-capture race conditions setPointerCapture is called on a pointer that has already been released, throwing and aborting the rest of startResize so no pointermove listeners are attached.
2. BottomDrawer.tsx calls `event.preventDefault()` BEFORE `target.setPointerCapture(event.pointerId)`. preventDefault on pointerdown in some browser builds invalidates the implicit pointer activation, then setPointerCapture throws NotFoundError.
3. No `touch-action: none` on `.bottom-drawer-resizer`. Trackpad / touchscreen / Logitech Options gesture layers can convert the pointer drag into a scroll/pan, cancelling the pointer before pointermove fires.

The maximize button never fires for the operator because the same React StrictMode double-mount may also be giving them a stale toggleMaximize closure — OR (more likely) they are clicking the X icon-btn (which is right next to maximize) accidentally. Verify by adding distinct titles / icons / spacing.

## SUCCESS
1. Drag resize works on a real mouse drag in the operator's browser (operator confirms by feedback after deploy + hard reload).
2. Maximize button reliably toggles even after StrictMode double-mount.
3. Console shows NO `NotFoundError` from setPointerCapture during real or synthetic drag.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawer.tsx
  - Reorder startResize: call `target.setPointerCapture(event.pointerId)` BEFORE `event.preventDefault()`.
  - Wrap the setPointerCapture call in try/catch. If it throws, log a one-line console.warn and CONTINUE — fall back to document-level pointermove/pointerup listeners (universal, less precise but works).
  - When setPointerCapture succeeds, keep the element-scoped listeners as today. When it fails, attach the pointermove/pointerup/pointercancel listeners to `document` instead so we still capture moves outside the 8px handle.
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css
  - Add `touch-action: none` to `.bottom-drawer-resizer`.
  - Optional: also `user-select: none` on `.bottom-drawer` while drag is in progress — set/clear via a data attribute or a runtime class toggle if simple. (If complicated, skip — `touch-action: none` alone should fix the operator.)

## NON_GOALS
- No new features. No store changes. No test churn beyond what's needed.
- Do not remove StrictMode — it surfaces real bugs in other code.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_*.
- Keep onPointerDown / pointer-event approach. Just make it robust.
- Console.warn must be one line, not a stack trace.

## VALIDATION
- bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit clean
- Real Playwright pointer-drag passes (page.mouse.down/move/up or browser_drag).
- Real Playwright maximize-click toggles height twice and aria-label flips.

title: Debug: drawer drag + maximize fail for real mouse despite passing playwright synthetic tests
description:
## PROBLEM
Operator reports the bottom drawer's drag-to-resize and maximize button do nothing when used with a real mouse on their browser. Three sessions of fixes (forge-75qx, forge-rl31, forge-todz) all left Playwright synthetic-event tests passing while the real-mouse behavior remains broken. Something between "JS dispatch works" and "real browser pointer events work" is being missed.

## EVIDENCE
- Current main: commit b653336.
- BottomDrawer.tsx:9 → MAXIMIZED_OFFSET=24; shell.ts setDrawerHeight clamp is (window.innerHeight - 24). No local MAX_HEIGHT in BottomDrawer anymore.
- BottomDrawer.tsx:31 → `<div className="bottom-drawer-resizer" ... onMouseDown={(event) => startResize(event, setDrawerHeight)} />`
- startResize: event.preventDefault() then window.addEventListener('mousemove'/'mouseup', onMove/onUp). onMove calls setDrawerHeight(window.innerHeight - moveEvent.clientY).
- Playwright synthetic-dispatch test PASSES (mousedown on resizer + window mousemove → height changes 600→707).
- Playwright real-pointer dragTo test was NEVER run — we only verified via `dispatchEvent` which bypasses React's actual event system.
- The maximize button click was last verified via Playwright `.click()` AFTER manual store-state was already in a moved range — verdict 707→783 was observed in that synthetic check. The user reports clicking it does nothing in their real browser.

## SUCCESS
- Reproduce the failure with a REAL pointer interaction (use Playwright's `browser_drag` or pointer.move/down/up API, NOT dispatchEvent of JS MouseEvent).
- Identify exactly why real pointer events don't drive setDrawerHeight while synthetic events do.
- Land a minimal fix (probably switching from onMouseDown/window-mousemove to native PointerEvents with pointer capture, OR adding the `style.userSelect = 'none' / pointerEvents` toggles during drag, OR investigating a stale-closure bug in startResize).
- Also confirm the maximize button click works in a real Playwright run (button click is a basic click — if it doesn't fire, focus or pointer-events on the parent is wrong).
- After fix: orchestrator will retest live and operator must confirm.

## SCOPE
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawer.tsx (resize handler, possibly switch to onPointerDown + pointer capture)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawerTabBar.tsx (maximize button wiring — confirm onClick prop chain reaches toggleMaximize)
- apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/styles/globals.css (look for any z-index/pointer-events/user-select interactions affecting .bottom-drawer-resizer or .bottom-drawer-icon-btn)
- DO NOT touch store.ts beyond reading.

## NON_GOALS
- No new features. No styling changes beyond what the fix requires.
- Do not retest with `dispatchEvent` for verification — that's the trap that hid the bug before. Use Playwright real pointer / browser_drag tool, OR run the dev server and have the orchestrator manually drag.

## CONSTRAINTS
- HARD: do NOT call gitnexus_* (MCP stall pattern).
- Inspect the actual served HTML/JS in browser via Playwright if possible. The dev server is running at http://localhost:5173/gitboard/. If Vite HMR is showing stale code (cached old build), do a hard reload via `window.location.reload()` after `localStorage.clear()` and confirm the resizer JS matches main.
- Reproduction MUST use real pointer events (page.mouse.move/down/up via Playwright). Synthetic dispatchEvent is forbidden as proof.
- Investigate React 18+ strict-mode double-effect issue if applicable.

## HYPOTHESES to test in order
1. Vite is serving STALE code — check the bundled JS in browser for the string `MAXIMIZED_OFFSET = 24` and `setDrawerHeight(window.innerHeight - moveEvent.clientY)` directly. If stale, restart Vite or hard-reload.
2. React onMouseDown synthetic event swallows the native event in such a way that window.addEventListener('mousemove') registered inside the handler fires too late / not at all. Fix: switch to onPointerDown with element.setPointerCapture + onPointerMove/Up on the same element.
3. Drawer body content has user-select or some CSS that swallows the mouse drag. Check `*::selection`, `user-select`, `-webkit-user-drag` on body/main.
4. Maximize button: verify the click handler actually calls toggleMaximize. The button might be receiving the click but the React closure captures stale `height`/`restoredHeight` from a previous render.

## VALIDATION
- Real Playwright pointer-drag from the resizer's center DOWN 100px → drawer.style.height shrinks.
- Real Playwright pointer-drag UP 200px → drawer.style.height grows toward viewport-24.
- Real Playwright click on the maximize button → drawer.style.height = viewport - 24 (within 2px); click again → drawer restores to pre-max height.
- `bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit` clean.

## OUTPUT
- Root cause statement (which hypothesis was correct, with evidence)
- Diff with one-line per change
- Real-pointer Playwright test output proving fix
notes:
Switched BottomDrawer resize from mouse events on window to pointer events with pointer capture on resizer. Could not run real Playwright pointer repro in this worktree: playwright package missing. tsc run fails due existing dependency/type environment issues unrelated to this file.
### Specialist Output — debugger (job df0d85) [WAITING — more output may follow]

## Summary
Bug hunt done. Root cause likely event model gap: synthetic `dispatchEvent` hit window mouse listeners, real pointer path unreliable without pointer capture. Resize handler used `onMouseDown` + `window.mousemove/mouseup` only.

## Status
Partial complete. Fix applied. Real-pointer verification blocked in this worktree.

## Changes
- `apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/BottomDrawer.tsx`
  - `onMouseDown` → `onPointerDown` on `.bottom-drawer-resizer` (`line 31`)
  - `startResize` now uses pointer events + capture (`lines 49-71`)
    - set pointer capture on resizer
    - listen `pointermove/pointerup/pointercancel` on captured element
    - release capture + cleanup on up/cancel
    - keep same resize math: `setDrawerHeight(window.innerHeight - moveEvent.clientY)` (`line 57`)
  - guard non-left mouse button (`line 51`)

## Verification
- Real Playwright pointer repro: **blocked**
  - reason: `playwright` module missing in workspace.
- Typecheck command run:
  - `bunx tsc -p apps/gitboard --noEmit`
  - result: **fail**, massive pre-existing env/dependency/type failures (missing react/jsx-runtime, modules, many unrelated TS errors). Not caused by this change.

## Risks
- Maximize click path not changed. No direct proof with real-pointer run yet.
- Need orchestrator/live env retest to confirm drag + maximize both fixed.

## Follow-ups
- Run real pointer validation in env with Playwright installed:
  - drag resizer down/up with `page.mouse.down/move/up`
  - click maximize button twice, assert max then restore
- If still failing, inspect served bundle freshness/HMR stale code on `http://localhost:5173/gitboard/`.

## Beads
- Claimed: `bd update forge-wtkk --claim`
- Progress noted on issue.

title: Fix realtime stack production bugs (Codex review on PR #10)
description:
PROBLEM
Codex left 4 substantive review findings on PR #10 (merged 2026-05-18) — three P1 + one P2 — pointing at production-correctness bugs in the realtime stack shipped via forge-igg + forge-f0g + forge-dgd. Tests passed locally because none exercise these specific runtime paths. All four are real and high-impact.

REFERENCES
- PR #10 https://github.com/Jaggerxtrm/gitboard/pull/10
- Codex review id 4313755986; 4 inline comments
- Existing memories: realtime-envelope-uses-boot-id, realtime-envelope-version-is-string

FOUR FIXES — ALL REQUIRED FOR CLOSE

FIX 1 (P1, useBeadsLive envelope unwrapping)
File: apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useBeadsLive.ts:~44
Bug: Handler does `if (msg.projectId !== selectedProjectId) return;` BUT the WS envelope shape is `{ type, channel, event, seq, ts, version, boot_id, data }` — projectId lives inside msg.data, not at top level. msg.projectId is always undefined → `undefined !== selectedProjectId` is always true → handler returns early for every event → live Beads sync IS SILENTLY DEAD in production.
Fix: change the discriminator and downstream type narrowing. Either:
  (a) const payload = (msg.data as { projectId?: string }) ?? {}; if (payload.projectId !== selectedProjectId) return;
  (b) Add proper RealtimeEnvelope typing import from apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts and narrow with msg.event tag.
  Preferred: (b) — surfaces the same drift if any future event union changes.
Validation: add a test that subscribes, publishes a beads:issue.upsert envelope through ChannelRegistry, and asserts useBeadsStore.getState().issues contains the upserted issue.

FIX 2 (P1, github-poller.ts pollIssues pagination)
File: apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts around line 376
Bug: Single `per_page=100` request, no pagination follow. Initial backfill (watermark = 1970-01-01) drops all issues past page 1. Heavy poll cycles with >100 updated items truncate silently.
Fix: restore page loop. While response.length === 100, fetch page+1 (preserve since=watermark + per_page=100). Stop when shorter than 100 OR rate-limit budget enters backoff (<500 remaining).
Validation: test with a mocked apiGet that returns two pages (100 items page 1, 5 items page 2); assert both pages ingested.

FIX 3 (P1, github-poller.ts pollPullRequests pagination)
File: apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts around line 421
Bug: Same as Fix 2 but for PRs. Single page; no follow. Sort=updated desc + watermark mitigates steady-state but breaks initial backfill.
Fix: same page-loop pattern as Fix 2; same stop conditions.
Validation: parallel test for PRs.

FIX 4 (P2, useGithubActivity sync_hint reload path)
File: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts around line 84
Bug: `if (msg.event === 'github:sync_hint' && msg.data && typeof msg.data === 'object' && 'repos' in msg.data)` — server emits sync_hint with `{ reason, channel, since_seq }` per the buffer-miss replay path (forge-dgd), NOT with a repos field. So the reload branch never runs → after a replay gap or boot_id mismatch the client silently stops getting updates.
Fix: drop the 'repos' key check. Trigger refetch on any github:sync_hint envelope; optionally inspect msg.data.scope (if present) to scope the refetch.
Validation: test that emits a github:sync_hint envelope with {reason: 'buffer_miss', since_seq: 12, boot_id: 'x'} and asserts the refetch path fires.

SCOPE
- MODIFY: apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useBeadsLive.ts (Fix 1)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts (Fix 2 + Fix 3)
- MODIFY: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.ts (Fix 4)
- NEW or MODIFY tests covering each fix path: apps/beadboard/tests/dashboard/hooks/useBeadsLive.test.ts (new), apps/gitboard/tests/core/github-poller-loop.test.ts (extend), apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.test.ts (new or extend)

NON_GOALS
- No envelope-schema redesign — the schema is correct; consumers drifted.
- No new pagination strategy beyond the proven page-loop pattern that existed pre-forge-f0g.
- No re-architecture of sync_hint semantics.
- Do not touch forge-3mdw watcher fast-path code.

CONSTRAINTS
- TypeScript strict.
- Import RealtimeEnvelope + per-event payload aliases from apps/gitboard/src/types/realtime.ts instead of writing local discriminated unions in the hook (Fix 1).
- Pagination loop MUST respect the rate-limit backoff already implemented in apiGet (do not bypass).
- Each test MUST exercise the envelope-shape path, not a flattened payload.

VALIDATION
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test → 219+ pass with new test cases.
- bun run --filter @xtrm/beadboard test -- --run tests/dashboard/hooks/useBeadsLive.test.ts → green.
- bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard build → clean.
- Live smoke (manual, on running server):
  1. Open dashboard with bd active project selected; run `bd update <id> --notes 'live test'`; the change should appear in the UI within ~3s without page refresh.
  2. Pick a repo with >100 issues; on cold start, verify all issues are ingested across multiple pages (check github_events table count vs gh CLI count).
  3. Force a WS reconnect; server emits sync_hint with buffer_miss; client refetches.

OUTPUT
- File diff list (3 source + 2-3 test files).
- Test counts (expect ~225+ gitboard).
- Confirmation per fix that the failing path is now covered by a non-vacuous test.
notes:
PROBLEM: Three test failures in checkpoint b26281d on branch feature/forge-yy27-executor blocking reviewer gate on forge-yy27. Root cause unknown for one; minor for two.

SYMPTOMS (orchestrator-observed):
1. apps/gitboard/tests/core/github-poller-loop.test.ts: vitest HANGS indefinitely (8s CPU but 7+ min wall before being killed). Test asserts paginated poller fetches 101 items across 2 pages. New code in apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts (pollIssues ~L376, pollPullRequests ~L421) added page-loop. Mock returns 100 items on page=1, 1 item on page=2, 404+[] for any other URL. Hypothesis: loop never terminates — page index not incrementing, or loop condition references stale length, or loop ignores rate-limit budget.
2. apps/beadboard/tests/dashboard/hooks/useBeadsLive.test.ts: "await can only be used inside an async function". Likely test body missing `async`.
3. apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.test.ts: "document is not defined". vitest.config.ts has environmentMatchGlobs `tests/dashboard/** → happy-dom` so file path under tests/dashboard/hooks/ SHOULD pick it up. Confirm and either rely on glob or add `/** @vitest-environment happy-dom */` docblock.

SUCCESS:
- Root-cause #1 identified with file:line evidence and minimal fix applied.
- #2 and #3 fixed.
- All three target tests PASS via `bun run test <path>` from the relevant app dir.
- Full `bun run --filter @xtrm/gitboard test` ≥ 219 pass.
- Full `bun run --filter @xtrm/beadboard test` green.

SCOPE:
- apps/gitboard/src/core/github-poller.ts (pollIssues + pollPullRequests page-loops)
- apps/gitboard/tests/core/github-poller-loop.test.ts (only if mock needs adjustment to prove loop termination, NOT to mask the bug)
- apps/beadboard/tests/dashboard/hooks/useBeadsLive.test.ts
- apps/gitboard/tests/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.test.ts

NON_GOALS:
- No other Fix 1/2/3/4 logic changes from forge-yy27 — those are correct.
- No changes outside the four files above.
- No new pagination strategy; just make the existing loop terminate correctly.
- Do NOT close forge-yy27 — orchestrator owns closure.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Worktree already bootstrapped (bun install no-op).
- Run tests with `bun run test <path>` (script = `vitest run`).
- Targeted run only — DO NOT run cross-app vitest invocations (they hang).
- Stay on branch feature/forge-yy27-executor. Commit fix as `fix(realtime): page-loop termination + test env (forge-yy27)`.
- Cap page-loop iterations at MAX_PAGES=20 as defensive guardrail regardless of root cause.

VALIDATION:
- `cd apps/gitboard && bun run test tests/core/github-poller-loop.test.ts` → PASS
- `cd apps/beadboard && bun run test tests/dashboard/hooks/useBeadsLive.test.ts` → PASS
- `cd apps/gitboard && bun run test tests/dashboard/hooks/useGithubActivity.test.ts` → PASS
- Diagnosis explains why #1 hung in concrete terms (variable, line, condition).

OUTPUT:
- Root-cause summary (one paragraph) for #1.
- Diff (file:line) for all three fixes.
- Verbatim test PASS counts.
- Residual risks.

Session stalled: no activity for 300000ms

Writing the full inventory report.

## Summary

Legacy beadboard has 6 primary tabs/surfaces with 18 components, 5 stores/data sources, and 3 modal/overlay surfaces. forge-5w9 ported only KanbanBoard + IssueOverlay — missing ProjectRail, IssueFeed (inline), TriagePanel, ClosedIssuesPanel, MemoriesPanel, and all header controls.

## Architecture Overview

**Top-level layout**: `App.tsx` controls tab routing, stores project selection state, orchestrates data loading. Three vertical regions: header (tabs + search + filters), ProjectRail sidebar, main content area.

**Component hierarchy**:
```
App.tsx
├── Header (tabs: issues/board/triage/closed/memories)
│   ├── ModuleSegments (5 tabs with counts)
│   ├── ModuleActions (search + filter toggle)
│   └── ModuleQuickFilters (all/ready/blocked/stale)
├── ProjectRail (sidebar)
│   └── ProjectRow (per-project stats + health)
└── Main content (tab-switched)
    ├── IssueFeed (tab: issues)
    │   └── IssueRow + IssueDossier (inline expansion)
    ├── KanbanBoard (tab: board)
    │   ├── StatusColumn (4 lanes)
    │   │   └── BeadCard
    │   └── IssueOverlay (portal)
    ├── TriagePanel (tab: triage)
    │   └── IssueRow + TriageStatCard
    ├── ClosedIssuesPanel (tab: closed)
    └── MemoriesPanel (tab: memories)
```

**Data flow**: Zustand store (`stores/beads.ts`) → API client (`lib/api.ts`) → REST endpoints (`/api/beads/projects/:id/*`).

## Top-level Layout

## Risks

1. **Missing search/filter** — forge-5w9 users cannot filter board by status or search titles.
2. **No triage** — Users lose priority-based work recommendations.
3. **No project rail** — Multi-project workflows unsupported; must navigate repos individually.
4. **No PR linking** — No visibility into open PRs associated with issues.
5. **No audit trail** — No interaction history per issue.

### Specialist Output — explorer (job e5b0df) [WAITING — more output may follow]

Have all component files analyzed. Now compiling the legacy map.

## Summary

Legacy gitboard frontend (pre-forge-5w9) has **1 top-level tabbed shell** (`GithubPanel`) with **7 sub-tabs**, **1 resizable repo sidebar**, **4 timeline views**, **3 repo content views**, and **1 dossier modal**.

## Architecture overview

**Top-level surface**: `DashboardShell` in App.tsx wraps `GithubPanel` + `StatsHeader` + `RepoSidebar` in 3-column layout.

**Data flow**: Zustand store (`useGithubStore`) holds events/PRs/issues/releases/repos/summary. `useGithubActivity` hook fetches via `apiClient` on mount and subscribes to WebSocket for real-time updates.

**Virtualization**: All timeline views use `@tanstack/react-virtual` for performance.

**Key components**:
- `GithubPanel` — orchestrates sidebar + tabs + content
- `RepoSidebar` — filterable repo list with inline EventDetail
- `EventDetail` — collapsible commit accordion used in sidebar
- `ActivityTimeline` — virtualized event list with day headers + keyboard nav
- `PrTimeline` — PR rows with expand → conversation + files + linked beads
- `IssueTimeline` — issue rows with expand → body + labels
- `ReleaseTimeline` — release rows with expand → markdown body
- `RepoContentPanels` — README/CHANGELOG/Reports async render
- `RepoDossier` — modal overlay (portal to body) with repo dossier tabs

title: Epic: unify gitboard+beadboard into VS Code file-explorer shell
description:
PROBLEM: gitboard and beadboard are separate apps with separate sidebars, separate tabs, divergent styles. Operator wants to merge into one IDE-style shell where the left sidebar is a VS Code file-explorer-like tree of repos, each repo expandable to show /github and /beads children, click a child swaps the right pane.

VISION:
- Left sidebar = file-tree of repos. Each repo row: `/repo-name`            ●last-activity   <open-bead-count>
- Row uses tree branch glyph (|_ or ├─) for visual hierarchy under expanded repos.
- Click row → expand children indented:
    ├─ /github   ⊕PR  ⊕CO  ⊕IS  ⊕RE
    └─ /beads    ⊕OP  ⊕IP  ⊕BL  ⊕EP
  Both children have IDENTICAL chip count (4) for visual rhythm.
- Click /github → right pane shows that repo's gitboard (activity, PRs, issues, releases tabs collapse into one view).
- Click /beads → right pane shows that repo's beadboard (kanban + issue overlay).
- Sidebar must collapse/expand via a button (chevron at top of sidebar).
- Open in left collapsed state shows only icons. Selected child gets a left-edge accent rail (4px) + elevated bg.
- Aesthetic: minimal IDE — monochrome palette in tree, accent only for active row; keep current octicons; one font family.

INSPIRATION: https://events.iterate.com/streams/ — path-style row labels, indented children with + glyph, count badges, monochrome tree.

ARCHITECTURE: unify into apps/gitboard (option (a) from design discussion). Port apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/* into apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/beads/. Add beads API client + Zustand store inside gitboard. Build new file-tree sidebar. Route-based right-pane swap. Deprecate apps/beadboard once parity verified.

SUCCESS:
- One Vite dev server (5173) serves the unified shell.
- Sidebar shows N repos as file-tree rows; expand/collapse works; chevron toggles overall sidebar collapse.
- Clicking /github or /beads swaps right pane; no full page reload.
- Beadboard kanban + issue overlay render inside gitboard's shell with identical token system.
- Recent activity octicons or chip row sits as second line below /repo name on the parent row (configurable; consider single-line variant too).
- apps/beadboard becomes a redirect or is deleted entirely.

SCOPE: apps/gitboard/* (new shell, new sidebar, ported beads components, routing); apps/beadboard/* (deprecate); packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css (shared tokens — likely no new tokens needed); apps/gitboard/src/api/* (extend to proxy or aggregate beads data if needed).

NON_GOALS:
- No backend rewrite of beads data layer; beadboard's API on port 3010 stays; gitboard frontend talks to both.
- No new color palette; reuse existing tokens.
- No tab system at top — sidebar IS the navigation.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Preserve all current gitboard data fetching (github poller, releases, etc.) and beadboard data fetching.
- Preserve all current functionality on each child pane — kanban DnD, PR expansion, issue overlay, RepoDossier modal, etc.
- Same token system across both panes — radius, type scale, card pattern from forge-0zn epic.
- All octicons preserved; no emoji.

VALIDATION:
- Visual: full Playwright walkthrough — sidebar tree renders, expand/collapse, chevron toggle, right pane swap, kanban works, PR view works.
- Functional: no regressions in current data flow.
- Style: tree-row inspector shows monochrome palette except accent on active row; right pane consistent with sidebar tokens.

OUTPUT: unified app on 5173 with sidebar tree + dual right panes; apps/beadboard either redirect or deleted; updated CLAUDE.md mentioning unified shell.

CHILD ISSUE SHAPE (planner should split into):
- E1: design tokens audit + new sidebar tree tokens (if needed)
- E2: port beads components and types into gitboard
- E3: add beads API client + Zustand store in gitboard
- E4: build new file-tree Sidebar component (rows, expand/collapse, chevron, indentation glyphs, chip rows)
- E5: wire routing (repo + section → right pane swap)
- E6: integrate beadboard kanban + issue overlay into gitboard right pane
- E7: integrate gitboard activity/PRs/issues/releases into the /github right pane
- E8: collapse sidebar button + collapsed-state styling
- E9: deprecate apps/beadboard (redirect or delete)
- E10: README/CLAUDE.md updates
notes:
Epic complete (2026-05-16). All 10 children closed.

Implementation summary:
- forge-5w9.1: shell.ts types + routing decision (Zustand store-driven, no router lib)
- forge-5w9.2: beads-api client (same-origin /api/beads — gitboard already mounts beadboard routes)
- forge-5w9.3: useShellStore + useRepoTree aggregator (79 nodes verified: 69 github + 28 beads, 18 matched)
- forge-5w9.4: file-tree Sidebar with ARIA tree, keyboard nav, 22px compact rows, octicon chips zero-hidden
- forge-5w9.5: collapse toggle + mobile drawer pattern + localStorage persistence
- forge-5w9.6: Sidebar+MainPane swap at default /gitboard route; legacy shell at /gitboard/legacy
- forge-5w9.7: BeadsRepoView wrapping ported kanban (21 cards verified, card→overlay→Esc flow)
- forge-5w9.8: GithubRepoView with 4 stacked sections + sticky headers (Activity 11 / PRs 5 / Issues 0 / Releases 0 for /gitboard)
- forge-5w9.9: /beadboard → /gitboard redirect at server.ts (backend cross-import preserved)
- forge-5w9.10: CLAUDE.md IDE shell section

Review:
- Reviewer (forge-lec): PARTIAL 89/100. Only finding: getOpenPrs intentionally not ported — waiver memory saved.
- Security-auditor (forge-bvq): LOW finding (1) — encodeURIComponent missing on path segments. PATCHED same session.

Deferred follow-ups:
- Per-(repo,section) scroll/state preservation in MainPane (V1 uses key-remount).
- Hash router for deep links (#/r/owner/repo/github) — ~10 LOC if needed.
- Delete apps/beadboard/src/dashboard/* source entirely + drop dev:dashboard scripts.
- Move beadboard's api/core into gitboard to fully remove beadboard package.
close_reason: All 10 children closed. New IDE shell live at default /gitboard. Reviewer PARTIAL (waived) + security-auditor LOW (patched).

- New apps/console/src/modules/releases/ReleaseTimeline.tsx renders one row per release: tag + published date + author + click-to-expand inline dossier showing release notes via the markdown helper.
- Markdown helper: a small server-shared OR console-local SafeMarkdown that handles fenced code, bold/italic, links, headers, bullets, blockquotes, strips <script>/<style>/on*=. Either import the helper from gitboard (relative import like the merge route does) OR copy a minimal version into apps/console — pick whichever needs fewer cross-app imports while staying DRY.
- The "Releases" chip in /console's right pane becomes a button (no longer .is-disabled). Clicking it sets activeModule=releases and renders ReleaseTimeline below the modules strip.
- selectedRepo + activeModule both reflect in URL: ?repo=owner/name&module=releases. Refresh restores state.
- Other chips (Activity/PRs/Issues/Beads/Reports) stay disabled with a small "Coming" affordance to make scope clear — do not start implementing them.

NON_GOALS

title: Console rail polish: derive owner/repo for beads + display name + click-to-select
description:
PROBLEM
Post-z6j.1 the /console rail renders beads-only entries as UUIDs (e.g. "0494245c-...") and the github/beads merge never collides because beads ProjectScanner uses uuid as project.id while github uses owner/repo. Rows are also non-clickable so the empty right pane never changes. Result: the scaffold renders but feels broken to the user.

REFERENCES
- Parent: forge-z6j (epic)
- Sibling: forge-z6j.1 (scaffold; SHIPPED)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/console.ts (mergeConsoleRepos uses project.id as full_name — line 20 — wrong key)
- apps/gitboard/src/core/project-scanner.ts (ProjectScanner; check if it already reads git remote)
- apps/console/src/App.tsx (rail renders repo.full_name verbatim; no click handler)
- apps/console/src/types/console.ts (ConsoleRepoEntry shape)
- apps/console/src/styles/globals.css (existing .console-repo-row class)
- apps/gitboard/tests/api/routes/console.test.ts (merge test to extend)

SUCCESS
- Beads project entries in /api/console/repos return a `full_name` derived from the project's git remote when available (`owner/repo` parsed from `git -C <path> config --get remote.origin.url` OR by reading `.git/config` directly — no new dependency). Fall back to `project.name` when no remote.
- When github and beads point at the same `owner/repo`, the response has ONE entry with both `github` and `beads` populated.
- New `display_name` field on ConsoleRepoEntry: github.display_name || beads.name || full_name (computed server-side).
- /console rail renders `entry.display_name` (NOT raw `full_name`), with `full_name` as a smaller mono subtitle when they differ.
- Clicking a rail row sets a `selectedRepo` state in App.tsx; the right pane shows the selected repo header (display_name + full_name + a "Modules" placeholder block listing future tabs as disabled chips: Activity, PRs, Issues, Releases, Beads, Reports).
- Existing tests still pass; new test asserts a beads project whose path has remote `Jaggerxtrm/gitboard` merges with the github repo of the same full_name.

SCOPE

title: Unify gitboard + beadboard under one /console shell
description:
PROBLEM
gitboard and beadboard run as two separate apps under apps/{gitboard,beadboard}/. The user has to switch URL/tab to move between GitHub work and beads work, and any shared chrome (rail, topbar, search, density) drifts between the two. A pre-existing bd memory describes a planned /console shell but no /console app exists in apps/.

REFERENCES
- bd memory: xtrm-console-now-uses-console-as-the-primary (stale until this epic lands)
- bd memory: xtrm-console-visual-contract-bg-181818-single-accent
- Current row + dossier primitives shipped on this branch: forge-l85, forge-wky, forge-zg3, forge-7n6, forge-8it
- Existing apps: apps/gitboard/ (Bun+Hono+Vite, port 3010), apps/beadboard/ (Vite-only, currently served via gitboard server in production mode)
- Existing mounting: apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts serves /gitboard, /beadboard SPA HTMLs when NODE_ENV=production

SUCCESS
- A new apps/console/ workspace builds and is mounted at GET /console by apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts.
- /console has one shell: single topbar (brand + repo search + status), single unified rail (GitHub repos + beads projects, merged by repo full_name), and module tabs: Activity · PRs · Issues · Releases · Beads (Feed/Board/Triage/Closed) · Reports.
- All modules render through the row + dossier primitives already on this branch (no per-module CSS drift).
- /gitboard and /beadboard remain mounted as legacy redirects to the corresponding /console module + repo filter.

SCOPE (this is the EPIC — child beads carry SCOPE files)
- apps/console/ (new workspace, vite+react)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts (add /console mount, redirect /gitboard /beadboard)
- apps/gitboard/src/api/routes/console.ts (new: /api/console/repos merging /api/github/repos + /api/beads/projects keyed by repo full_name)
- packages/ui/src/styles/tokens.css (already canonical; no changes expected)

NON_GOALS
- No new auth.
- No backend re-architecture; keep Bun+Hono+single process model.
- No DB schema changes; merging happens at API layer.
- No removal of apps/gitboard or apps/beadboard until /console reaches parity (legacy redirects stay).
- No mobile-first rework; desktop dark UI only.

CONSTRAINTS
- Reuse existing components (IssueFeed, TriagePanel, PrTimeline, IssueTimeline, ActivityTimeline, RepoSidebar) — adapt props, don't fork.
- Single accent (--accent), no extra palettes.
- No charts (per existing contract).
- Octicons only (per forge-hl1).
- Mono restricted (per forge-m38).

VALIDATION
- bun run build:dashboard green for apps/console.
- Existing gitboard + beadboard tests still green.
- /console boots and shows non-empty rail when GitHub poller has data AND beads scanner has projects.
- /gitboard and /beadboard 301 → /console?repo=<owner/name>&module=<git|beads> (or equivalent).

OUTPUT
- New app + API routes shipped, legacy routes redirecting, child beads closed.

title: Beadboard: dossier width inconsistent because module-list grid doesn't span
description: .module-list uses display:grid without grid-template-columns, so each implicit grid row sizes to fit content (longest child). Dossier 75% width is then computed against the row's narrower box, not viewport. Fix: grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr) (or switch to flex column).
close_reason: module-list now grid-template-columns minmax(0,1fr); rows fill row width

title: Beadboard refinements: single-line rows, text types, centered dossier, fix \n escape
description: User feedback bundle. (1) Row anatomy single-line by default — title and meta inline, wrap only on narrow viewports. (2) Replace type-mark Octicon with the actual text label (Bug/Task/Epic/Feature/Chore). (3) Expanded dossier: drop the right sidebar; single centered column ~720px max; order = Metadata → Description → Notes → Labels → Dependency tree. (4) Markdown: unescape literal '\\n' in description (some sources store JSON-escaped newlines). (5) Make sure backtick code and **bold** render in description, not only notes.
