forge-5w9.3 shell store shape: useShellStore (zustand) holds repos:RepoNode[], expanded:Set<string>, selection:SidebarSelection|null, sidebarCollapsed:boolean. All three of expanded/selection/sidebarCollapsed persist to localStorage under forge-5w9:expanded/selection/sidebarCollapsed keys. Narrow selectors (selectActiveSection, selectRepos, selectExpanded, selectSidebarCollapsed) avoid re-renders. Aggregator hook useRepoTree() in apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/hooks/useRepoTree.ts: matches beads projects to github repos via TAIL of full_name (e.g. Jaggerxtrm/foo ↔ beads project 'foo'). 18 matched out of 69 github + 28 beads on this machine.

forge-f6qk.1 IA restructure: Surface union narrowed to github|console; ConsoleTab expanded to 6-tab strip via CONSOLE_TABS in types/shell.ts; legacy persisted surface='beads' migrated via (raw.surface as string) === 'beads' check in App.tsx (TS2367 fix); placeholders Graph.tsx and Specialists.tsx use DotFillIcon + 'Coming in forge-f6qk.{3,5}' copy with ide-empty/ide-console-placeholder classes. Pattern: reviewer first-passed against EPIC validation instead of child bead — rebut by citing child bead VALIDATION explicitly.

forge-f6qk.2 graph backend: GET /api/console/graph mounted in api/server.ts; new files core/graph-dao.ts + api/routes/graph.ts + types/graph.ts + tests/api/routes/graph.test.ts. resolveProject returns null on missing project_id (NOT first-project fallback) — empty payload with 'project' note. 9 edge types verbatim in types/graph.ts:3 union. Live specialists filter to {starting,running,waiting}. Reviewer rebuttal pattern: cite the actual union enumeration as structural proof rather than expecting manual smoke.

gitboard realtime WebSocket server expects client messages with action=subscribe/unsubscribe (not type). If live views only refresh on navigation, check shared WsClient message shape and mounted view subscriptions to beads:changes.

Lesson from live e2e: F8 SpecialistOwnerBadge was wired into BeadCard.tsx but the IDE shell renders rows through IssueFeed.tsx (article.row class), not BeadCard. ALWAYS verify which component actually paints the row by inspecting DOM classNames in the browser before declaring binding work done. The 'bead row' isn't always BeadCard — IssueFeed has its own article element.

Realtime envelope version is STRING not number — initial forge-dgd shipped with version:number + Date.parse coercion, which silently fell back to REALTIME_PROTOCOL_VERSION for non-numeric strings (e.g. Dolt commit hashes), breaking dedupe for beads. Caught during forge-f0g review. Now: version is the source's row updated_at ISO (github) or commit hash (beads), passed through verbatim. Default for unversioned publishes is String(REALTIME_PROTOCOL_VERSION).
Density pass: shrink spacing by token-level padding/gap changes, not font size; keep row height via --row-height and topbar via --topbar-height.

gitboard already mounts beadboard's /api/beads/* routes via apps/gitboard/src/api/server.ts:6,35 (imports beadsRoutes from '../../../beadboard/src/api/routes/beads.ts'). Cross-package import. forge-5w9.9 deprecation MUST move beadboard's api/routes/beads.ts + core/beads-*.ts into apps/gitboard before deleting beadboard, or gitboard's beads endpoints break. beadboard backend on port 3010 is NOT a runtime dependency for gitboard — gitboard serves beads itself on its own port (3000).

Observability metrics-dao token_trajectory_json: entries are {t, source/turn_index, token_usage: {input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_creation_tokens, cache_read_tokens, total_tokens, cost_usd}}. The token fields are NESTED under .token_usage, not top-level. Easy gotcha when summing tokens.

Custom <button> elements rendered as chips/pills MUST reset background to transparent + font: inherit + border-radius; browser user-agent button bg renders as a light block in dark-themed UIs and looks broken next to <span> siblings

When sp reviewer or sp merge complains about huge scope ('540 files changed') vs a much smaller claimed scope, suspect a stale local main: the merge-base used by the reviewer is whatever's in your local refs, and if local main is far behind origin/main (or behind a recently-merged PR), the cumulative diff balloons. Workaround: ignore the scope-breach finding when you can confirm the executor's own diff (git log <branch> ^HEAD --oneline) is small, and patch-apply the executor's actual commits directly. Source: forge-5wc and forge-pq6 reviewers both flagged 540-file scope while real executor diffs were 2 and 3 files.
forge-7ap: canonical tokens stay in packages/ui; gitboard active chrome and beadboard chrome should reference vars only, with preview sandbox styles treated separately

forge-5w9.6 MainPane swap: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/MainPane.tsx reads useShellStore selection. key={repo:section} on <main> forces clean remount on swap. EmptyState shows 3 most recently active repos as clickable cards. /gitboard default route uses ShellApp (Sidebar + MainPane). /gitboard/legacy preserves old TabBar shell. Per-(repo,section) state preservation deferred — currently lost on swap via key-remount.

forge-5w9 reviewer waiver (forge-lec): getOpenPrs intentionally NOT ported into beadsApi. Rationale: it's a /api/github/prs endpoint, not /api/beads. gitboard's own apiClient.getPrs covers github PR fetches. prByIssueId is OPTIONAL in BeadCard/StatusColumn/KanbanBoard component props — never wired = no runtime regression. Future PR-link feature should reshape apiClient.getPrs() result into OpenPr at the wiring layer, not duplicate the fetcher in beads-api. Reviewer score 89/PARTIAL — accepted with this waiver.

sp merge fails with bookkeeping conflicts when the specialist's executor branch is based on a snapshot where .beads/ files don't exist as currently tracked. Workaround: git cherry-pick -X theirs <commits>, then 'git checkout HEAD -- .beads/' to restore bookkeeping, then 'git rm --cached' any '.beads~<hash>' conflict-marker entries before committing. The executor's design changes land clean; only .beads/ infra needs preservation. Source: forge-93p merge session.

forge-qc0 layout invariant: ANY MainPane content that uses @tanstack/react-virtual (ActivityTimeline, PrTimeline, IssueTimeline, ReleaseTimeline) needs a SIZED parent. Required CSS chain: html/body/#root height:100%; .ide-shell height:100vh + overflow:hidden; .ide-body flex:1 + min-height:0; .ide-main flex:1 + min-height:0 + overflow:hidden; .ide-main > * flex:1 + min-height:0. Without this chain virtualizer reports container as zero-height and renders nothing. min-height:100vh on shell is NOT enough — must be height:100vh.

Gitboard global theme is controlled by ShellState.theme persisted in localStorage key forge-vk47:theme and applied via .ide-shell[data-theme=light|dark]; keep dark default and preserve borderless feed/list rows when tuning light mode.

renderPrBodyText only handled links + inline code originally; added **bold** + *italic* + .pr-rich-text h3/p/ul/li/strong/em/code styling to make GH release/PR notes render with proper visual hierarchy. Regex uses negative lookbehind `(?<![*\\w])` to prevent italic from eating bold markers.
terminal e2e review: verify base branch naming (master vs main) before hard-gating scope checks

forge-0w4 backend gotchas: (1) /api/beads/projects/{id}/issues/closed returns 404 on Dolt-backed projects — fetch ALL via /issues?limit=1000 and split client-side. (2) /api/github/events default limit is 50 (most-recent across ALL repos), /api/github/prs is 100 — client-side filter-by-repo on the global store returns 0 for most repos. Solution: per-repo apiClient.getEvents({repos:[fullName]}) + getPrs({repo}) + getIssues({repo}) + getReleases({repo}) in a local hook (useGithubRepoData in MainPane.tsx).
forge-f6qk.4 review: missing explicit onClose callback in BeadSideDrawer despite close paths working

forge-f6qk.6 console epic test coverage: 6 new test files (ia-restructure, Graph, BeadSideDrawer, Specialists, useChains, fixtures/console-graph.json) + 13 tests. Pattern: tests fork off origin/main but need post-integration state — workflow is push xt/integration branch, dispatch with --force-stale-base, executor first git fetch origin <branch> && git reset --hard origin/<branch>. Reviewer pitfall: diff source defaults to main; for branches reset to non-main, rebut with explicit base (git diff --name-only origin/<branch>..feature/<x>).

gitboard sidebar Beads source chip: useRepoTree polls /api/beads/projects/:id/connection and maps connected->dolt, jsonl_fallback->jsonl; /connection now validates Dolt by running a tiny getIssues query, not just opening a socket.
GitHub Contents API: GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path} returns base64 file content for files OR array of entries for dirs. Decode with Buffer.from(content, 'base64'). 404 means missing file.
Beadboard kanban dossier markdown should strip raw HTML tags and whitelist link protocols before rendering inline.

Test-bead executor scope discipline lesson from T1: when dispatching executor on a test-only bead, the contract MUST say 'test files + fixtures ONLY — never edit production code. If a test fails because production code is wrong, REPORT do not edit.' Without this hard rule, executor expands scope to make tests pass. Also: never tell executor 'git checkout origin/main -- <file>' unless the file exists on origin/main — when local main is ahead of origin (common in auto-mode), this command deletes the file. Use 'git checkout main -- <file>' for local refs.

Observability persistence note (per forge-gud9 design): specialist_jobs rows in observability.db are RETENTION-FRIENDLY — sp keeps closed/error rows after a chain finishes. To surface a per-bead specialist history view, the only new API surface needed is GET /api/specialists/jobs?bead_id=…&include_history=1 returning all rows (live+closed) DESC by updated_at_ms. No new write path or migration needed; data is already there.

Unified expanded-row card pattern: when .pr-row.is-expanded (or .issue-row.is-expanded), strip bottom radius+border on row and zero its margin-bottom; on the .pr-expanded-body (or .issue-expanded-body), strip top border+radius. Result: row+body render as a single card with full rounded outer corners only. Required because the row and body are separate DOM siblings — without this they look like two stacked cards.
bv triage score uses pagerank, staleness, priority_boost, time_to_impact, urgency, and risk; top picks can be mirrored client-side with open issue idle time, priority cost, and open load.

Code-sanity specialist must be dispatched with --job <exec-job>, never standalone. Its registry entry is worktree:none, so a solo dispatch has no filesystem to read and returns 'BLOCKED — source not retrievable' (confirmed on forge-zgny job a937b8). Correct invocation: chain off an executor's worktree via 'sp run code-sanity --bead <id> --job <executor-job-id>'. For ad-hoc sanity passes without a prior executor, run a no-op executor first to provision a worktree, then attach code-sanity via --job.

Gitboard Beads Feed UI rules: active issues render blocker tree with single primary blocker placement to avoid duplicate rows; epic relation propagates purple markers through nested descendants; right column format is 'P<n> TYPE status date / deps / PR / agent' with only priority/type colored by type (bug red, epic purple, feature royal blue).
forge-3ue5.8 review 5de5b7: owner badge/hook/tests implemented; requirements mostly met; PARTIAL due to missing storybook/visual evidence and failing test runtime deps.

Gitboard Beads Feed now uses compact multiline rows with relationship groups on the second line; Beads tabs are Feed/Triage/Memories only; Feed sections are in_progress, collapsible open expanded by default, and collapsed closed.

forge-gud9 implementation: VSCode-style bottom drawer mounted as sibling of MainPane content inside .ide-main. Uses zustand shell.ts drawer state (drawerOpen/drawerHeight 120-600/drawerTab) persisted via forge-gud9: localStorage keys. Tab bar with Logs + Specialists tabs, terminal-style rows. Logs tab uses useSystemLogs hook subscribing to useWebSocket('system', ...) → 2000-entry ring + level chip filter + search + autoscroll-on-tail + clear button + W/E count status. Specialists tab uses useAllSpecialistJobs polling /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight every 5s returning {in_flight, recent_history}. Backend added recentJobs(limit) DAO method + extended /jobs/in-flight to return both buckets. Theme tokens: --bottom-drawer-bg/--bottom-drawer-tabbar-bg/--bottom-drawer-border at :root with .ide-shell[data-theme=light] override. Ctrl/Cmd+J global keydown handler in MainPane. Collapsed by default to prevent first-load layout shift. @tanstack/react-virtual used in Logs panel when entries >= 500. Live e2e via Playwright confirmed: 31 in_progress + 50 history rows, tab switching, theme toggle, ctrl+j toggle all work. Reviewer PASS after rebuttal evidence on wrapper test path.

Specialist reviewers in gitboard validate their own worktree, not uncommitted main-worktree diffs; for self-implemented changes, commit or provide a branch-visible artifact before dispatching reviewer, otherwise reviews fail on missing lineage/diff evidence.

gitboard bottom drawer logs are backed by core/logger ring + system WebSocket channel; useSystemLogs must fetch /api/internal/logs on mount because ws client.connected is emitted before the system subscription exists and old ring entries otherwise never appear.

forge-f6qk.3 graph frontend: layered LR layout in pages/console/graph/layout.ts with 9 edge weights (blocks:5, supersedes:3, parent-child:0.5, discovered-from:2, validates:2, caused-by:2, tracks:1, until:1, related:0.2); pulse filter MUST be status==='running' only (not full live set); types/graph.ts at apps/gitboard/src/types/graph.ts (NOT pages/console/graph/types.ts — placeholder pattern at fork-from-stale-main is fine but orchestrator must rewire imports + delete placeholder at integration). MainPane Graph route does NOT pass repo prop (component reads projectId from useShellStore selection).
PR detail view now uses section-label + file-row patterns; linked beads extracted from forge-* refs
Beadboard dossier audit log now fetches interactions lazily on expansion; stale quick filter uses 7-day updated_at threshold.

CSS grid implicit-column trap: display:grid WITHOUT grid-template-columns creates an implicit single-column grid sized to fit-content, not fill the parent. Children shrink to the width of the LARGEST row content, not to the container. Symptom: percentage widths on grandchildren resolve inconsistently because the parent row is variable width. Fix: always specify grid-template-columns (e.g. minmax(0, 1fr)) on a single-column list grid, OR use display:flex; flex-direction:column. Source: forge-l85 beadboard .module-list inconsistent dossier width.

forge-f6qk.4 bead side-drawer: new BeadSideDrawer + useBeadSideDrawer hook; mounted in App.tsx as sibling of MainPane; IssueFeed row-click opens drawer (inline expand preserved); ESC + backdrop + X-button all fire optional onClose callback. z-index 50 (BottomDrawer is 40). Scope discipline pattern: when children fork off main without prior sibling's diff, executor re-does sibling's work — orchestrator must steer/resume with explicit revert: git checkout main -- <files>.

forge-yy27 production-bug fix root causes: (1) useBeadsLive read projectId from envelope top-level instead of msg.data — live sync silently dead until unwrap. (2) test mock used url.includes('page=N') which COLLIDES with 'per_page=100' substring causing infinite loops in pagination tests — always use new URL(url).searchParams.get('page') for accurate page matching. (3) getIssues/getPrs in github-store default LIMIT=100 — pagination tests must pass {limit: 200} or higher. (4) vitest happy-dom is the canonical jsdom-like env in this repo (jsdom package not installed). Use @vitest-environment happy-dom docblock, not jsdom.
Audit forge-fje bounded row-normalization failed: build:dashboard missing vite in current scope and required reviewed_job_id not injected

Beads UI affordance gaps vs bd CLI: 'bd sync' command does NOT exist (design buttons referencing it must use bd import or be dropped); bd does support comments, search, defer/undefer, reopen, create, history, graph — these are absent from the xtrm-console design but should be added when the UI lands. Source: forge-tm0 explorer audit job e75d94.

BottomDrawer resize must be defensive: (1) setPointerCapture in try/catch — React StrictMode double-mount can leave the pointer with no active ID by the time the second invocation runs, throwing NotFoundError; (2) on catch, fall back to document.addEventListener for pointermove/pointerup so drag still works; (3) call setPointerCapture BEFORE preventDefault — preventDefault on pointerdown can invalidate implicit pointer activation; (4) .bottom-drawer-resizer needs touch-action: none in CSS or trackpad/touchscreen gestures convert drag into scroll and cancel the pointer before pointermove fires.

Live e2e proof for forge-3ue5 (gitboard ownership view): with sp jobs running in 2+ repos, /api/specialists/jobs/in-flight returns 6 rows tagged by repoSlug (gitboard, xtrm-tools), and InFlightRail renders them grouped by repo with elapsed time. Screenshot: apps/gitboard/.playwright-mcp/e2e-rail-live.png
Review run: 14d1e1 compliance audit completed with traceability evidence and build verification

BeadsChangeWatcher fast-path (forge-3mdw): cache lastCommitHash per project; if cheap getCommitHash() matches last seen AND we have a previous snapshot, emit beads:source_health and return WITHOUT readSnapshot. Drops poll-tick cost from a full N+1 hydration (~600 queries for ~200 issues) to a single SELECT current_commit() (~2ms). DoltClient.getIssues + getClosedIssues use hydrateIssues(rows) which does 1 issues SELECT + 3 IN-clause batched queries (dependencies/issue_related/labels) instead of per-row sub-queries. issue_related/labels remain optional (try/catch swallow); they're absent from some Dolt schemas.

forge-a72 tsc cleanup: 28→0 errors via mechanical fixture/type-cast updates. Concrete patterns: RepoStat test fixtures need issues_open/releases/last_event_at; dolt-client mysql2 overloads need 'as unknown as' casts; bun:sqlite bindings tighten via explicit row types; happy-dom (not jsdom) for hook tests; Record<string,unknown>[] casts via 'as unknown' for typed array→generic-record conversions; null-guard string params before encodeURIComponent.

forge-zpw8 logger: structured emit() + LogEntry types + WS fan-out on 'system' channel + JSONL daily disk sink with 7-day retention. Singleton at apps/gitboard/src/core/logger.ts. Call sites in poller (cycle/rate_limit/etag), watcher (poll.skipped/snapshot_read/source_health.changed/batch.published/drift.detected), dolt-client (breaker.opened/query.slow/reconnect.attempt/recovered), ws/handler (client.connected/disconnected/subscribe.version_mismatch), server (request.slow/error). Endpoints: GET /api/internal/logs (ring query, level/component/event/since/limit), /api/internal/logs/files (list jsonl files).
review forge-m38 fail when mono appears on non-identifier UI text in globals

sp merge fails with conflict on .beads/issues.jsonl when the orchestrator session mutates main's bd state (create child beads, add deps, claim, close) while a specialist worktree is open. Worktree forked from old main + main advanced + both touched issues.jsonl = unresolvable merge. Skill's chain-noise-filter applies to manual cherry-pick flows, NOT to sp merge — sp merge bails. Mitigations: (a) batch all orchestrator bd writes before dispatching the next chain, (b) commit + push main's bd state between dispatches so worktrees fork from current state, or (c) fall back to manual cherry-pick with --strategy-option=ours on .beads/. Discovered swarm-wide on forge-3ue5.1 dispatch.

sp's observability.db at <repo>/.specialists/db/observability.db is the right join source for bead↔specialist UI in gitboard (not 'sp ps' shell-out). specialist_jobs has bead_id+specialist+status+chain_id+epic_id+chain_kind+worktree_column+last_output, indexed by bead_id and chain_id. Open mode=ro, pin schema_version, mutations stay out of v1.

Specialist chip visibility rule (per operator design feedback): chip surfaces ACTIONABLE state only. Show running/starting/waiting (live in-flight) + error/cancelled (failure traceability — operator needs to know which specialist died). HIDE 'done' — a successful past run is history, not an actionable signal, and belongs in the per-bead dossier timeline (forge-4hmt). Don't bloat the feed with success badges. Rule lives in SpecialistOwnerBadgeForBead.VISIBLE_STATES + useSpecialistOwnership fallback chain (live → error → cancelled, never done).

Drawer height/resize was visually broken because .ide-main > * { flex: 1 } in globals.css overrode all inline style.height on direct children of MainPane (including BottomDrawer). Fix: explicit .ide-main > .bottom-drawer { flex: 0 0 auto; } — opts the drawer out so its pixel-height wins. CRITICAL TEST RULE for any height-driven UI primitive in this app: verify rendered height via getBoundingClientRect().height, not element.style.height. Playwright reads inline style and gives false-positive when flex layout silently overrides.
xtrm-console tokens now centralized in packages/ui; app chrome imports canonical tokens and square badge radii use var(--radius-sm)/var(--radius-pill)=0

CSS grid + position:absolute child gotcha: an absolutely-positioned child of a 'display: grid' container is REMOVED from grid flow, so its template column gets filled by the NEXT in-flow child. If your design has N grid-template-columns but you include a position:absolute decoration as one of the spans, you actually have N-1 in-flow children filling N columns — everything shifts left and columns visually misalign (e.g. id renders in the rail slot, title renders in the id slot). Two fixes: either drop the absolute span (use a ::before pseudo instead) or keep it in-flow with grid-column:1. Source: forge-pq6 beadboard rows where <span className='rail' /> with position:absolute caused id+title to overlap until forge-xsk.

Wave-8 T3 Playwright deferred: forge-3ue5.13 not closed in v1 auto-mode run. Trigger condition: when repo-wide vitest infra (@vitejs/plugin-react workspace dep) is fixed, dispatch T3 executor with test-only scope discipline. Bead state preserved with notes.
Beadboard project summaries now carry source health and priority; issue detail endpoint falls back from Dolt to sqlite when live Dolt unavailable.

Theme gotcha: gitboard's --accent-blue is defined ONCE in :root (#7ed7e5/#8ed2dc pale cyan) and NOT overridden in [data-theme=light]. Anything using it directly will wash out on the white background. PR#10 left this asymmetry. When building a new themed UI element that needs a vibrant accent on BOTH themes, define your own component-scoped token at :root + override in .ide-shell[data-theme='light'] (pattern: see --specialist-chip-accent in globals.css). Theme-aware tokens that ARE properly overridden: --text-muted, --text-secondary, --text-primary, --accent-dim, --accent-soft, --bg-hover.

Dolt connection stability (forge-1qz): dolt-client.ts uses mysql2/promise createPool with health-probe (SELECT 1 every PROBE_INTERVAL_MS), per-query QUERY_TIMEOUT_MS=3s wrapper, and a circuit breaker (BREAKER_THRESHOLD consecutive failures opens it; probe success closes it). The probe-then-retry pattern IS the half-open mechanism — no separate state machine. BeadsChangeWatcher.readIssues checks isBreakerOpen() and skips Dolt for JSONL when open. Health metrics exposed at /api/internal/dolt-health (no credentials/query bodies leaked).

Beadboard /api/beads/projects/<id>/issues returns BeadDependency entries with title='' (the reader/exporter doesn't denormalise dep titles). Clients must resolve dep titles by id from the loaded issues list rather than trusting dep.title. Same for dep.status — prefer issueById.get(dep.id)?.status. Source: forge-3k5 dependency tree fix.
gitboard and beadboard dashboard builds may need bun install when vite bin missing in workspace

Gitboard expanded-body card pattern: wrap .pr-expanded-body and .issue-expanded-body with --card-border, --card-radius-md (8px), --surface-secondary bg, 14-18px padding, small 4-6px margin. This creates GitHub-style 'card floating on darker page' effect. Page bg --surface-primary (#0d1117) is darker than --surface-secondary (#161b22), so the card pops. Watch for later .pr-expanded-body override rules in globals.css (~line 1974) — must keep them aligned with first rule (~line 1819).
Terminal protocol validator must enforce capabilities enum values, not string[] only; otherwise unsafe capabilities drift.

Reviewer rebuttal pattern: when reviewer flags 'unmet: parameterized queries' but actual interpolation is only schema identifiers (table names, column aliases), rebut by citing SQLite's hard limitation that table names/aliases cannot be parameter-bound. The contract constraint about 'parameterize bead/chain/epic ids' specifically named user-data ids — schema identifiers from PRAGMA database_list and registry-controlled slugs are not in that category. Cite file:line of the ? placeholders and the db.prepare(query).all(...params) call. Rebuttal accepted on forge-3ue5.4 (81→94).

gitnexus_context() MCP call stalls explorers — confirmed twice on forge-k4zy (job 7d51c7 and 9f93e8) at gitnexus_context({name: 'watcher'}). The call hangs past 120s heartbeat → sp marks the job errored. Symptom: explorer shows 'current: gitnexus_context' in sp ps but tokens stop advancing. Workaround: in bead CONSTRAINTS, instruct explorer to use serena find_symbol / get_symbols_overview + grep instead of gitnexus_context for symbol mapping. Affects all explorers/debuggers dispatched on this repo until gitnexus MCP is fixed.

Real-world sp observability.db quirks discovered during live e2e (forge-xu1h test): (a) the slug derivation MUST strip the '.specialists/db' suffix from observability.db's parent — basename naively gives 'db' for every repo. (b) sp's schema_version values vary wildly across repos (0, 11, 68, 84, 26997) — a strict COMPATIBLE_RANGE rejects everything. Best contract check is table-shape presence (specialist_jobs exists), not version number. (c) Real observability.db paths live at both <repo>/.specialists/db/observability.db (modern) AND <repo>/.specialists/observability.db (legacy specialists repo) — the scan walks recursively which handles both.

Reuse existing .pr-body-text + .pr-rich-text CSS for any markdown body rendered via renderPrBodyText — those classes carry the white-space:pre-wrap, font-size, line-height baseline that browser defaults need to look right. Custom .release-body-text style produced unstyled markdown output (no h3/list/p spacing).

Beadboard descriptions sometimes contain literal JSON-escape sequences (the two characters backslash+n) instead of real newlines, because some bd callers (planner specialists, copy-pasted prompts, mol templates) store JSON-encoded content as the description verbatim. Any markdown renderer must run a replace(/\\n/g, '\\n')-style unescape before splitting on newlines or the body renders as a single line with visible backslash-n. Source: forge-zg3 description rendering fix.
Octicon consistency pass: replace decorative glyphs in dashboard meta lines with LinkExternalIcon/CheckIcon/ChevronDownIcon; keep design-preview svg diagram as non-icon exception.

forge-7xu unified shell: TopBar [GitHub|Beads] switch + tabs (7 github / 5 beads) + Sidebar single-level repos with RepoIcon + MainPane per-(surface,tab) dispatch. All 5 beads panels live in BeadsRepoView.tsx (Kanban=KanbanBoard, Feed=IssueFeed, Triage=inline TriagePanel ported from beadboard App.tsx:411, Closed=inline list, Memories=inline list). GitHub README/CHANGELOG/Reports use ReadmeView/ChangelogView/ReportsView from RepoContentPanels.tsx. CSS class prefix .ide-* in globals.css. Black bg via .ide-shell.

gitboard specialist live status fix: useSpecialistOwnership must poll mounted bead cards, and specialists router default DAO must refresh observability DB discovery because worktree orchestrators can create/write .specialists/db/observability.db after server startup.

terminal-stream-epic-design: For Gitboard embedded terminal, keep TerminalStream renderer separate from TerminalSessionProvider ownership; use versioned generic envelope with terminal-specific payloads; land protocol + safety gates before PTY; keep tmux/SSH/specialist-feed as follow-ups/non-MVP unless explicitly enabled.

Drawer/panel styling rule for gitboard UI: ANY new panel surface MUST use --surface-primary (= #000 dark / #fff light) — NEVER define custom drawer-bg-* or panel-bg-* gray tokens. Layered grays read as '3D' and clash with the rest of the app's plain flat aesthetic. Visual dividers between surfaces should be a single 1px hairline in --border-subtle that lights up to --accent on interaction (e.g. resize handle). Hit areas for thin dividers must be ≥6px (use transparent padding around a 1px ::before line) — 2px is mouse-ungrabbable in practice.

bd dep rule: an epic cannot block a task (bd dep add <epic-id> <task-id> errors with 'epics can only block other epics, not tasks'). Use --parent on bd create instead to nest tasks under an epic, OR use bd dep relate for non-blocking links between epic and task. Tasks can block tasks. Epics can block epics. Source: forge-qct epic linking session.

forge-5w9.4 sidebar component: apps/gitboard/src/dashboard/components/shell/Sidebar.tsx. Flat visible-row list approach for ARIA tree (no recursion). focusedId state synced via onFocus handler — critical to avoid stale-state when DOM focus moves out-of-band (e.g. tests calling .focus()).
